name: tcpdump
title: tcpdump
description:
command-line network traffic analyzer
This program allows you to dump the traffic on a network. tcpdump
is able to examine IPv4, ICMPv4, IPv6, ICMPv6, UDP, TCP, SNMP, AFS
BGP, RIP, PIM, DVMRP, IGMP, SMB, OSPF, NFS and many other packet
types.
.
It can be used to print out the headers of packets on a network
interface, filter packets that match a certain expression. You can
use this tool to track down network problems, to detect attacks
or to monitor network activities.

command_examples: tcpdump host helios and \( hot or ace \)
tcpdump ip host ace and not helios
tcpdump 'gateway snup and (port ftp or ftp-data)'
tcpdump ip and not net localnet
tcpdump 'tcp[tcpflags] & (tcp-syn|tcp-fin) != 0 and not src and dst net localnet'
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use tcpdump because command-line network traffic analyzer.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use tcpdump as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `tcpdump [ -AbdDefghHIJKlLnNOpqStuUvxX# ] [ -B buffer_size ]` as documented upstream for tcpdump.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `tcpdump host sundown` as documented upstream for tcpdump.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `tcpdump host helios and \( hot or ace \)` as documented upstream for tcpdump.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `tcpdump ip host ace and not helios` as documented upstream for tcpdump.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair tcpdump with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `tcpdump --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

examples: tcpdump host helios and \( hot or ace \)
tcpdump ip host ace and not helios
tcpdump 'gateway snup and (port ftp or ftp-data)'
tcpdump ip and not net localnet
tcpdump 'tcp[tcpflags] & (tcp-syn|tcp-fin) != 0 and not src and dst net localnet'
documentation.readme: tcpdump - dump traffic on a network
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install tcpdump. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `tcpdump [ -AbdDefghHIJKlLnNOpqStuUvxX# ] [ -B buffer_size ]`. Further invocations from upstream: `tcpdump host helios and \( hot or ace \)`; `tcpdump 'tcp[tcpflags] & (tcp-syn|tcp-fin) != 0 and not src and dst net localnet'`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: command-line network traffic analyzer.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: command-line network traffic analyzer.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install tcpdump
